title: coccothrinax miraguama subsp. cupularis
intro: Coccothrinax miraguama subsp. cupularis, also known as the Cuban thatch palm, is a subspecies of palm tree native to Cuba. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, derived from its leaves and sap. The health benefits of Coccothrinax miraguama subsp. cupularis include relief from fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to boost the immune system. Traditionally, the plant is used to make herbal preparations such as teas and infusions, which are used to treat various ailments, and it has also been used in folk medicine to make topical applications for skin conditions.
